concept: Articulate / Express Clearly / Give Voice To
description: To express thoughts or feelings with clarity and precision, giving clear voice to what might otherwise remain vague or unspoken. 'Moreko' names the skill of making inner experience communicable—finding words that accurately convey meaning.
sound_symbolism: The word shapes meaning clearly. 'Mo' begins with nasal depth, drawing from inner understanding. Then 're' rolls outward with extending expression, meaning moving toward speech. Finally 'ko' defines with velar clarity, words precisely formed.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a transitive verb: 'mia lo remo moreko.' (I articulate thoughts.) Related to 'mishoa' (express) and 'sheori' (communicate) but emphasizes clarity and precision.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Articulating experience helps us understand it—putting inner states into words brings awareness to what we feel and think.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Clear articulation prevents misunderstanding—when we express ourselves precisely, others can respond to what we actually mean.
